# PART I—INFORMATION REQUIRED IN THE PROSPECTUS

## PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

This summary highlights selected information contained elsewhere in this prospectus and does not contain all of the information you should consider before investing in our Class A common stock. You should read this entire prospectus carefully, including the sections titled "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus. Unless the context otherwise requires, references in this prospectus to "Crestline," the "Company," "we," "us," and "our" refer to Crestline Software, Inc. and its consolidated subsidiary.

### Our Company

Crestline Software, Inc. is an enterprise software-as-a-service ("SaaS") company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. We were incorporated in the State of Delaware on March 14, 2017. Our principal executive offices are located at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759. We operate through our wholly-owned subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales, which supports our European operations, and we maintain offices in San Francisco, California; London, United Kingdom; and Bangalore, India.

Our platform consists of the Crestline Nexus unified SaaS platform and the proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, which combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence, including demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing. As of December 31, 2024, we served 487 customers, including 34 customers contributing annual recurring revenue ("ARR") in excess of $1.0 million, and had 1,247 employees. Our intellectual property portfolio includes 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents. We achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in August 2024.

### Our Strengths

- **Leading AI-driven supply chain optimization platform.** We are AI-native from inception, with the proprietary SupplyMind AI engine protected by a robust patent portfolio.
- **Strong revenue growth and highly recurring revenue model.** Total revenue grew from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, an increase of 45.3%. Subscription revenue represented 90.0% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024, and ARR was $211.6 million as of December 31, 2024.
- **Best-in-class net retention.** Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024.
- **Path to profitability.** We generated Adjusted EBITDA of $20.5 million in fiscal year 2024. See "Selected Financial Data" and "Non-GAAP Financial Measures."
- **Diversified customer base.** No single customer accounted for more than 10% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024; our largest customer represented approximately 8.2% of total revenue.

### Our Growth Strategies

- Expand our enterprise customer base, leveraging our direct sales motion targeting Fortune 1000 accounts.
- Deepen existing customer relationships through cross-sell of additional SupplyMind modules, targeting net retention above 130%.
- Accelerate international go-to-market in EMEA and APAC, supported by our London and Bangalore offices.
- Continue platform innovation, including next-generation autonomous supply chain capabilities.
- Pursue opportunistic, tuck-in acquisitions of complementary AI/ML technology and talent.

### Implications of Being an Emerging Growth Company

We are an "emerging growth company" as defined in the JOBS Act. As an emerging growth company, we may take advantage of specified reduced reporting and other regulatory requirements that are not otherwise available to other public companies. These exemptions include: (i) presenting only two years of audited financial statements and two years of related Management's Discussion and Analysis; (ii) reduced disclosure about our executive compensation arrangements; (iii) exemption from the requirement to obtain an attestation from our independent registered public accounting firm on our internal control over financial reporting under Section 404(b) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002; and (iv) an extended transition period for complying with new or revised accounting standards. We could remain an emerging growth company for up to five years following the completion of this offering.

## RISK FACTORS

An investment in our Class A common stock involves a high degree of risk. Before investing, you should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described below, together with all of the other information contained in this prospectus. The occurrence of any of the following risks could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects, and the trading price of our Class A common stock could decline, and you could lose all or part of your investment.

### Risks Related to Our Business and Industry

**We have a history of net losses, may not achieve or sustain profitability, and may not generate sufficient cash to service our indebtedness.** We have incurred net losses since inception. We reported a net loss of $24.8 million and $27.4 million for the fiscal years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We may continue to incur net losses for the foreseeable future, and we may not achieve or sustain profitability.

**Our professional services operations are currently margin-negative.** For fiscal year 2024, our professional services revenue was $18.7 million and our professional services cost of revenue was $18.8 million, resulting in a negative professional services gross margin of approximately (0.5)%. Although we expect professional services gross margins to improve as implementation processes scale and standardize, there can be no assurance that professional services will become profitable or that we will achieve our target professional services gross margins of 15% to 20%.

**We depend on a limited number of customers for a significant portion of our revenue.** Our top 10 customers represented approximately 31.7% of our total revenue in fiscal year 2024, and our largest customer, Harmon Industries Inc., represented approximately 8.2% of our total revenue. The loss of, or a material reduction in purchases by, one or more of these customers could materially and adversely affect our revenue and results of operations.

**We face intense competition and may lack sufficient resources to remain competitive.** The market for supply chain optimization software is competitive, with well-capitalized incumbents and point-solution vendors. We may not be able to compete effectively.

**We are subject to intellectual property litigation that could materially and adversely affect our business.** On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. However, an adverse judgment could result in damages that our outside patent counsel currently estimate could range from $5.0 million to $20.0 million, and could result in injunctive relief requiring us to redesign or cease offering certain features. In addition, an adverse judgment in excess of $5.0 million that remains undischarged for 60 days could constitute an event of default under our term loan credit agreement, which could result in the acceleration of our indebtedness. See "Risk Factors—Risks Related to Our Indebtedness" and "Description of Indebtedness."

**Our intellectual property is pledged as collateral under our credit agreement.** Substantially all of our assets, including our intellectual property (consisting of our 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, 8 issued foreign patents, and our registered trademarks "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind"), are pledged as collateral to secure our obligations under our term loan. In the event of a default, the lender could foreclose on our intellectual property, which would severely and adversely affect our business.

**We depend on key personnel, including our co-founders.** Our success depends on the continued service of our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy, our Chief Executive Officer, and Katerina [●] Litvak, our Chief Technology Officer. The loss of the services of either co-founder could adversely affect our business. In addition, the departure of Mr. Ramaswamy as Chief Executive Officer could constitute a "change of control" under our term loan credit agreement unless a successor reasonably acceptable to the lender is appointed within 90 days, which could result in the acceleration of our indebtedness. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**If we fail to maintain effective internal control over financial reporting, we may not be able to accurately report our financial results.** As an emerging growth company, we are not required to obtain an attestation from our independent registered public accounting firm on our internal control over financial reporting. If we fail to maintain effective internal controls, we could be subject to regulatory scrutiny and a loss of investor confidence.

**Our ability to use our net operating loss carryforwards and certain other tax attributes may be limited.** As of December 31, 2024, we had approximately $228.0 million of federal net operating loss ("NOL") carryforwards and approximately $166.0 million of state NOL carryforwards. Our ability to use these NOLs may be limited if we experience an "ownership change" as defined in Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code, including in connection with this offering or other equity transactions. We have not yet completed a Section 382 study.

**We have identified a related party customer relationship that requires ongoing disclosure.** Vantara Logistics, Inc., a portfolio company of Graymont Ventures (our largest pre-offering stockholder), is a customer under a SaaS license agreement entered into in April 2023 with an annual contract value of $1.2 million and a three-year term. Priya Venkatesh, a member of our Board of Directors, is the Managing Partner of Graymont Ventures and serves on the board of Vantara Logistics. This transaction was reviewed and approved by our Audit Committee, with Ms. Venkatesh recused. See "Certain Relationships and Related Party Transactions."

### Risks Related to Our Indebtedness

**Our term loan credit agreement contains restrictive covenants and events of default that could limit our flexibility and result in acceleration of our indebtedness.** Our term loan with Kestridge West Credit Partners requires us to maintain minimum liquidity of $30.0 million and an ARR-based leverage ratio not exceeding 2.0x. The credit agreement also contains a change-of-control provision, a key-person provision relating to our Chief Executive Officer, a requirement that we provide the lender with at least 30 days' prior written notice before any public offering, and a judgment default threshold of $5.0 million. Our failure to comply with these covenants could result in an event of default and the acceleration of our indebtedness, which would materially and adversely affect our financial condition. See "Description of Indebtedness."

### Risks Related to Our Class A Common Stock and This Offering

**Because of the ten-to-one voting ratio of our Class B common stock, our co-founders will control a majority of the combined voting power of our capital stock and will be able to control all matters submitted to a vote of our stockholders.** Our co-founders will hold all of our outstanding Class B common stock, representing approximately 71.8% of the combined voting power of our outstanding capital stock immediately following this offering. As a result, our co-founders will have the ability to control the outcome of all matters submitted to a vote of our stockholders, including the election of directors, amendments to our certificate of incorporation, and approval of significant corporate transactions. This concentrated voting control will limit the ability of holders of our Class A common stock to influence corporate matters.

**Our Class B common stock has no sunset provision.** Our Class B common stock will continue to have ten votes per share indefinitely, and will convert into Class A common stock only upon transfer to a non-permitted transferee or upon the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based or ownership-based sunset. As a result, the concentration of voting control with our co-founders will continue indefinitely. Certain major stock index providers may exclude companies with multi-class voting structures from their indices, which could limit the demand for, and the market price of, our Class A common stock.

**We are a "controlled company" under Nasdaq listing rules and may rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements.** Because our co-founders will control more than 50% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock, we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq listing rules. As a controlled company, we may elect to rely on exemptions from the Nasdaq requirements that a majority of our board of directors be comprised of independent directors, that we have a fully independent compensation committee, and that we have a fully independent nominating committee. As of the date of this prospectus, our Board of Directors consists of [●] members, [●] of whom are independent. If we rely on these exemptions, you will not have the same protections afforded to stockholders of companies that are subject to these corporate governance requirements.

**Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws contain provisions that may discourage acquisition proposals and limit the ability of stockholders to influence corporate matters.** These provisions include a classified (staggered) board of directors, the ability of the Board of Directors to fill vacancies, the elimination of the ability of holders of Class A common stock to act by written consent, limitations on the ability of stockholders to call special meetings, advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations, a requirement of a 66⅔% supermajority vote to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws, and director removal only for cause and upon a 66⅔% vote.

**Our exclusive forum provisions may limit the ability of stockholders to bring claims in certain judicial forums.** Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation provides that, unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act. These provisions may limit the ability of stockholders to bring claims in state court or in the courts of their choosing.

**You will experience immediate and substantial dilution.** The assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated price range) is substantially higher than the pro forma net tangible book value per share of our Class A common stock immediately following this offering. As a result, you will experience immediate and substantial dilution of $19.85 per share. See "Dilution."

**Future sales of our Class A common stock, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.** Substantial shares of our Class A common stock will be eligible for sale following the expiration of the 180-day lock-up period, including shares issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options (14,750,000 options at a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share) and the vesting of outstanding RSUs (3,200,000 RSUs). In addition, holders of our preferred stock have registration rights commencing 180 days after this offering. These sales could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.

**We do not intend to pay dividends on our Class A common stock.** We have never declared or paid any cash dividends on our capital stock. We do not anticipate paying any cash dividends in the foreseeable future. In addition, our term loan credit agreement restricts our ability to pay dividends or repurchase equity without the lender's consent. See "Description of Indebtedness" and "Dividend Policy."

## USE OF PROCEEDS

We are offering [●] shares of our Class A common stock. We estimate that the net proceeds from this offering will be approximately $[●] million (or approximately $[●] million if the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full), based on an assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated price range), after deducting the underwriting discount of 6.5% and estimated offering expenses payable by us of approximately $4.8 million.

The principal purposes of this offering are to increase our capital flexibility and financial position, to create a public market for our Class A common stock, and to facilitate our future access to the public equity markets. We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital, research and development, sales and marketing, and potential strategic acquisitions or investments. A portion of the net proceeds may be used to repay indebtedness under our existing term loan facility with Kestridge West Credit Partners, under which $40.0 million is currently outstanding, maturing December 2027, bearing interest at SOFR + 3.25%. We have not yet determined the specific amounts we intend to allocate to each of these uses. Accordingly, our management will have broad discretion in the allocation of the net proceeds. See "Risk Factors—Risks Related to Our Class A Common Stock and This Offering—You will experience immediate and substantial dilution."

We are required to provide Kestridge West Credit Partners with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of this offering, including the anticipated offering date, anticipated gross proceeds, anticipated use of proceeds, and a pro forma capitalization table. See "Description of Indebtedness."

Pending application of the net proceeds as described above, we intend to invest the net proceeds in a variety of capital-preserving investments, including short-term, investment-grade, interest-bearing instruments and U.S. government securities.

The initial public offering price of our Class A common stock will be determined by negotiations between us and the representatives of the underwriters. ## DILUTION

The net tangible book value of our Class A common stock as of December 31, 2024 was approximately $(3.29) per share. Net tangible book value per share is determined by dividing our total tangible assets (total assets of $240.1 million less intangible assets of $4.1 million) less total liabilities of $143.1 million, by the number of shares of common stock outstanding as of December 31, 2024 (52,000,000 shares), resulting in net tangible assets of $92.9 million, or $1.79 per share.

After giving effect to (i) the automatic conversion of all 35,416,667 shares of outstanding redeemable convertible preferred stock into shares of Class A common stock on a one-for-one basis and the reclassification of $385.0 million from mezzanine equity to permanent stockholders' equity, and (ii) the sale of 15,000,000 shares of Class A common stock in this offering at an assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share, after deducting the underwriting discount and estimated offering expenses, our pro forma net tangible book value as of December 31, 2024 would have been approximately $4.15 per share. This represents an immediate increase in pro forma net tangible book value of $3.09 per share to existing stockholders and immediate dilution of $19.85 per share to new investors purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering, as illustrated in the following table:

| | |
|---|---|
| Assumed initial public offering price per share | $24.00 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering | $1.06 |
| Increase per share attributable to this offering | $3.09 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering | $4.15 |
| Dilution per share to new investors | $19.85 |

If the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering would be approximately $4.36, and the dilution to new investors would be approximately $19.64 per share.

The foregoing dilution analysis does not give effect to (i) 14,750,000 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon exercise of outstanding stock options at a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share, (ii) 3,200,000 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, (iii) 550,000 shares remaining available for future grant under our 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, and (iv) 2,500,000 shares reserved under our 2024 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. To the extent these options or RSUs are exercised or vest, or additional awards are granted, there will be further dilution to new investors.

## PLAN OF DISTRIBUTION

We have entered into an underwriting agreement with Pinnacle Ridge Capital, as representative of the several underwriters, and Stonebridge Greer & Co., pursuant to which we have agreed to sell to the underwriters the shares of Class A common stock offered by this prospectus. The underwriting agreement will provide that the obligations of the underwriters are subject to customary conditions, including the effectiveness of the registration statement, the receipt of comfort letters from our independent auditor, opinions of counsel, and no material adverse change in our business.

The underwriters are offering the shares of Class A common stock, subject to receipt and acceptance, and subject to the underwriters' right to reject any order in whole or in part. The underwriting discount is 6.5% of the gross proceeds, allocated as a management fee of 20%, an underwriting fee of 20%, and a selling concession of 60%.

**Over-Allotment Option.** We have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2,250,000 shares of Class A common stock (15% of the base offering) at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discount, solely to cover over-allotments, if any.

**Stabilization.** The underwriters may engage in over-allotment sales, stabilizing transactions, and penalty bids in accordance with Regulation M under the Exchange Act. These activities may have the effect of raising or maintaining the market price of our Class A common stock above independent market levels or preventing or retarding a decline in the market price.

**Lock-Up Agreements.** Each of our executive officers, directors, and stockholders beneficially owning 5% or more of our outstanding capital stock immediately prior to this offering has agreed, subject to customary exceptions, not to offer, sell, or otherwise dispose of any shares of our capital stock or securities convertible into or exercisable for our capital stock for a period of 180 days following the date of the final prospectus, without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital. The lock-up agreements must be executed by the applicable fund-level entities that hold the shares, and not merely by the individual directors who serve as representatives on our Board. See "Certain Relationships and Related Party Transactions" and "Principal Stockholders."

**Listing.** We have applied to list our Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "CRST."

## DESCRIPTION OF CAPITAL STOCK

The following description summarizes the material terms of our capital stock. This description is qualified in its entirety by reference to our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and amended and restated bylaws, each of which will become effective immediately prior to the closing of this offering, copies of which are filed as exhibits to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

### General

Upon the closing of this offering, our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will become effective. It will authorize 1,500,000,000 shares of capital stock, consisting of 1,200,000,000 shares of Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; 200,000,000 shares of Class B common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; and 100,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.0001 per share.

### Conversion of Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock

Upon the closing of this offering, all outstanding shares of our Series A, Series B, Series C, and Series D redeemable convertible preferred stock (an aggregate of 35,416,667 shares) will automatically convert into shares of Class A common stock on a one-for-one basis, and the $385.0 million aggregate liquidation preference will be reclassified from mezzanine equity to permanent stockholders' equity. No shares of preferred stock will remain outstanding.

### Class A Common Stock

Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to one vote per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders. Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to receive dividends when, as, and if declared by our Board of Directors, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock. Upon any liquidation, dissolution, or winding up, holders of Class A common stock are entitled to share ratably in the assets remaining after payment of liabilities and any preferred stock amounts, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock.

### Class B Common Stock

Holders of Class B common stock are entitled to ten votes per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders. Class B common stock is held exclusively by our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy (12,200,000 shares) and Katerina [●] Litvak (8,600,000 shares), for an aggregate of 20,800,000 shares. Each share of Class B common stock is convertible at any time at the option of the holder into one share of Class A common stock, and will automatically convert into one share of Class A common stock upon (i) transfer to any person or entity other than a permitted transferee, or (ii) the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based or ownership-based sunset on the Class B common stock. Other than with respect to voting and conversion, the rights of Class B common stock are identical to those of Class A common stock.

### Preferred Stock

Our Board of Directors is authorized to issue shares of preferred stock in one or more series and to fix the designations, powers, preferences, and rights of each series without further stockholder approval. The issuance of preferred stock could adversely affect the voting power of holders of Class A common stock and could be used as a method of discouraging, delaying, or preventing a change of control.

### Registration Rights

Holders of our outstanding preferred stock (which will convert into Class A common stock in this offering) are parties to an Investor Rights Agreement dated June 2023, pursuant to which they are entitled to demand registration rights and S-3 piggyback registration rights commencing 180 days after the completion of this offering, subject to the terms and conditions of the agreement. A copy of the Investor Rights Agreement is filed as an exhibit to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

### Anti-Takeover Provisions

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws contain provisions that may discourage, delay, or prevent a change of control, including: a classified board of directors with staggered three-year terms; the ability of the Board of Directors to fill vacancies; the elimination of the ability of holders of Class A common stock to act by written consent; limitations on the ability of stockholders to call special meetings; advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations; a requirement of a 66⅔% supermajority vote to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws; and director removal only for cause and upon a 66⅔% vote. Our certificate of incorporation also renounces certain corporate opportunities that may be presented to directors who are affiliated with investment funds, including Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners.

### Exclusive Forum

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation provides that the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and that the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act, in each case unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum.

## DESCRIPTION OF INDEBTEDNESS

The following is a summary of the material terms of our term loan credit agreement with Kestridge West Credit Partners. This summary is qualified in its entirety by reference to the credit agreement, a copy of which is filed as an exhibit to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

In [●] 2022, we entered into a senior secured term loan facility with Kestridge West Credit Partners providing for borrowings of up to $50.0 million. As of December 31, 2024, $40.0 million was outstanding under the facility. The facility matures in December 2027 and bears interest at a variable rate equal to SOFR plus 3.25%, subject to a SOFR floor of 1.00%. The facility amortizes in quarterly installments, with the remaining balance due at maturity. An exit fee of 1.00% of the outstanding principal is due upon repayment in full or at maturity.

**Security and Guarantee.** Our obligations under the credit agreement are secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including our accounts receivable, intellectual property, equipment, deposit accounts, and equity interests in our subsidiaries (limited to 65% of the voting equity of our foreign subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd.). Crestline Software UK Ltd. provides a limited guarantee of our obligations, capped at £5.0 million.

**Financial Covenants.** We are required to maintain minimum liquidity (cash, cash equivalents, and eligible short-term investments) of not less than $30.0 million, and an ARR-based leverage ratio (total funded debt divided by ARR) not exceeding 2.0x, in each case tested quarterly. As of December 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all financial covenants, with liquidity of $137.0 million and a leverage ratio of approximately 0.19x.

**Change of Control.** A "change of control" under the credit agreement includes, among other things, the existing stockholders ceasing to hold at least 50% of the aggregate voting power of our outstanding capital stock, including following an initial public offering, and the cessation of Arun Ramaswamy as Chief Executive Officer unless a successor reasonably acceptable to the lender is appointed within 90 days. Based on the dual-class voting structure, our co-founders will retain approximately 71.8% of the voting power following this offering, and we do not believe this offering will trigger a change of control.

**Public Offering Notice.** We are required to provide the lender with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering, including the anticipated offering date, anticipated gross proceeds, anticipated use of proceeds, and a pro forma capitalization table. Failure to provide this notice within the required timeframe constitutes an event of default, subject to a 10-business-day cure period.

**Other Covenants and Events of Default.** The credit agreement contains customary affirmative and negative covenants, including limitations on additional indebtedness (exceeding $5.0 million without consent), liens, investments, restricted payments (including dividends and stock repurchases), and mergers and acquisitions. Events of default include cross-default to other indebtedness exceeding $2.0 million and judgments for the payment of money in excess of $5.0 million that remain undischarged for 60 days. Upon an event of default, the lender may accelerate all outstanding obligations and exercise remedies against the collateral.

## MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following discussion and analysis should be read together with our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus. This discussion contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Our actual results could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements.

### Overview

We are an enterprise SaaS company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform. We generate revenue from (i) subscription fees for access to our cloud-based platform, recognized ratably over the contractual term (typically one to three years), and (ii) professional services, recognized as services are delivered. For fiscal year 2024, subscription revenue was $168.6 million (90.0% of total revenue) and professional services revenue was $18.7 million (10.0% of total revenue). Total revenue was $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, an increase of 45.3% from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023. As of December 31, 2024, ARR was $211.6 million and our dollar-based net retention rate was 132%.

We have incurred net losses since inception. For fiscal years 2024 and 2023, we reported net losses of $24.8 million and $27.4 million, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We have funded our operations primarily through the issuance of redeemable convertible preferred stock (an aggregate of $385.0 million across four series) and, to a lesser extent, revenue from operations and borrowings under our credit facility.

**Revenue.** Total revenue increased $58.4 million, or 45.3%, from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, driven by new customer acquisition and expansion within our existing customer base. Subscription revenue increased $52.2 million, or 44.8%, and professional services revenue increased $6.2 million, or 49.6%.

**Gross Profit and Gross Margin.** Gross profit increased $43.5 million, or 49.7%, from $87.6 million in fiscal year 2023 to $131.1 million in fiscal year 2024. Gross margin increased from 67.9% to 70.0%. Subscription gross margin was 77.8% in fiscal year 2024. Professional services gross margin was approximately (0.5)% in fiscal year 2024, as professional services cost of revenue ($18.8 million) exceeded professional services revenue ($18.7 million).

**Operating Expenses.** Total operating expenses increased $40.4 million, or 34.0%, from $118.7 million in fiscal year 2023 to $159.1 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily due to increased headcount and stock-based compensation. Stock-based compensation expense was $38.2 million and $26.4 million in fiscal years 2024 and 2023, respectively. Fiscal year 2024 also included $1.2 million of restructuring charges related to a workforce reduction affecting approximately 46 employees in the third quarter of 2024.

**Net Loss.** Net loss decreased $2.6 million, from $27.4 million in fiscal year 2023 to $24.8 million in fiscal year 2024, reflecting improved operating leverage.

Adjusted EBITDA is not a substitute for GAAP net loss. Investors should be aware that our definition of Adjusted EBITDA may not be comparable to similarly titled measures used by other companies.

## BUSINESS

### Overview

Crestline Software, Inc. is an enterprise SaaS company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. Our platform, Crestline Nexus, is powered by the proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, which combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence.

### Our Products

**Crestline Nexus Platform.** A unified SaaS platform for end-to-end supply chain optimization, with real-time data ingestion from ERP, WMS, TMS, and IoT systems, pre-built connectors for over 100 enterprise data sources, and a multi-tenant, cloud-native architecture.

**SupplyMind AI Engine.** Proprietary machine learning models for demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing, protected by our patent portfolio.

### Market Opportunity

We estimate the total addressable market for AI-driven supply chain software to be approximately $28 billion by 2028, with a serviceable addressable market of approximately $9 billion focused on Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. We believe current penetration is less than 3% of the serviceable addressable market.

### Customers

As of December 31, 2024, we served 487 customers across manufacturing, retail, logistics, consumer packaged goods, and other industries. Our top 10 customers represented approximately 31.7% of fiscal year 2024 revenue, and our largest customer represented approximately 8.2% of fiscal year 2024 revenue. No single customer accounted for more than 10% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024 or 2023. Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024.

### Intellectual Property

As of December 31, 2024, our intellectual property portfolio consisted of 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents. Our material trademarks include "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind," each registered with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. We achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in August 2024. We are subject to the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) through our UK subsidiary and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA).

### Employees

As of December 31, 2024, we had 1,247 employees.

### Properties

We lease our principal executive offices in Austin, Texas (our headquarters, expiring August 2029, with annual base rent of approximately $3.8 million), and additional offices in San Francisco, California (expiring March 2027, annual base rent approximately $2.1 million); London, United Kingdom (expiring January 2028, annual base rent approximately £680,000); and Bangalore, India (expiring June 2026, annual base rent approximately INR 42,000 thousand).

### Legal Proceedings

On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. The lawsuit is in the early stages, and no trial date has been set. We have not recorded a loss contingency for this matter because management believes it is not probable that a loss has been incurred. Based on the advice of outside patent counsel, we estimate the potential range of loss, in the event of an adverse judgment, to be between $5.0 million and $20.0 million, although an unfavorable outcome is considered to have a low probability. An adverse judgment in excess of $5.0 million that remains undischarged for 60 days could constitute an event of default under our term loan credit agreement.

**Vantara Logistics, Inc.** In April 2023, we entered into a SaaS license agreement with Vantara Logistics, Inc., a portfolio company of Graymont Ventures, our largest pre-offering stockholder. The agreement provides for annual subscription fees of $1.2 million over a three-year term, for a total contract value of $3.6 million. The transaction was reviewed and approved by our Audit Committee, with Ms. Venkatesh recused from the deliberation and vote. The Audit Committee concluded that the terms were comparable to those offered to similarly situated customers. Revenue recognized from Vantara was $1.2 million during fiscal year 2024 and $0.9 million during fiscal year 2023. Accounts receivable from Vantara as of December 31, 2024 and 2023 were $0.3 million and $0.25 million, respectively.

**Preferred Stock Financings.** Graymont Ventures participated in our Series A (October 2018), Series B (April 2020), and Series D (June 2023) financing rounds, and Ridgeline Growth Partners participated in our Series C (November 2021) and Series D (June 2023) financing rounds, in each case on terms negotiated at arm's length and approved by our Board of Directors.

**Investor Rights Agreement.** In June 2023, we entered into an Investor Rights Agreement, Voting Agreement, and Right of First Refusal and Co-Sale Agreement with certain preferred stockholders in connection with the Series D financing. # PART I—INFORMATION REQUIRED IN THE PROSPECTUS

## PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

This summary highlights selected information contained elsewhere in this prospectus and does not contain all of the information you should consider before investing in our Class A common stock. You should read this entire prospectus carefully, including the sections titled "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus. Unless the context otherwise requires, references in this prospectus to "Crestline," the "Company," "we," "us," and "our" refer to Crestline Software, Inc. and its consolidated subsidiary.

### Our Company

Crestline Software, Inc. is an enterprise software-as-a-service ("SaaS") company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform to Fortune 1000 manufacturers and retailers. We were incorporated in the State of Delaware on March 14, 2017. Our principal executive offices are located at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759. We operate through our wholly-owned subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales, which supports our European operations, and we maintain offices in San Francisco, California; London, United Kingdom; and Bangalore, India.

Our platform consists of the Crestline Nexus unified SaaS platform and the proprietary SupplyMind AI engine, which combines machine learning, advanced statistical modeling, and real-time data integration to deliver predictive and prescriptive supply chain intelligence, including demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics routing. As of December 31, 2024, we served 487 customers, including 34 customers contributing annual recurring revenue ("ARR") in excess of $1.0 million, and had 1,247 employees. Our intellectual property portfolio includes 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, and 8 issued foreign patents. We achieved SOC 2 Type II certification in August 2024.

### Our Strengths

- **Leading AI-driven supply chain optimization platform.** We are AI-native from inception, with the proprietary SupplyMind AI engine protected by a robust patent portfolio.
- **Strong revenue growth and highly recurring revenue model.** Total revenue grew from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, an increase of 45.3%. Subscription revenue represented 90.0% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024, and ARR was $211.6 million as of December 31, 2024.
- **Best-in-class net retention.** Our dollar-based net retention rate was 132% for fiscal year 2024.
- **Path to profitability.** We generated Adjusted EBITDA of $20.5 million in fiscal year 2024. See "Selected Financial Data" and "Non-GAAP Financial Measures."
- **Diversified customer base.** No single customer accounted for more than 10% of total revenue in fiscal year 2024; our largest customer represented approximately 8.2% of total revenue.

### Our Growth Strategies

- Expand our enterprise customer base, leveraging our direct sales motion targeting Fortune 1000 accounts.
- Deepen existing customer relationships through cross-sell of additional SupplyMind modules, targeting net retention above 130%.
- Accelerate international go-to-market in EMEA and APAC, supported by our London and Bangalore offices.
- Continue platform innovation, including next-generation autonomous supply chain capabilities.
- Pursue opportunistic, tuck-in acquisitions of complementary AI/ML technology and talent.

### Implications of Being an Emerging Growth Company

We are an "emerging growth company" as defined in the JOBS Act. As an emerging growth company, we may take advantage of specified reduced reporting and other regulatory requirements that are not otherwise available to other public companies. These exemptions include: (i) presenting only two years of audited financial statements and two years of related Management's Discussion and Analysis; (ii) reduced disclosure about our executive compensation arrangements; (iii) exemption from the requirement to obtain an attestation from our independent registered public accounting firm on our internal control over financial reporting under Section 404(b) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002; and (iv) an extended transition period for complying with new or revised accounting standards. We could remain an emerging growth company for up to five years following the completion of this offering.

### Corporate Information

We were incorporated in Delaware on March 14, 2017. Our principal executive offices are located at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759. Our telephone number is (512) [●]. Our website is [●]. Information contained on, or accessible through, our website is not a part of this prospectus.

## RISK FACTORS

An investment in our Class A common stock involves a high degree of risk. Before investing, you should carefully consider the risks and uncertainties described below, together with all of the other information contained in this prospectus. The occurrence of any of the following risks could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects, and the trading price of our Class A common stock could decline, and you could lose all or part of your investment.

### Risks Related to Our Business and Industry

**We have a history of net losses, may not achieve or sustain profitability, and may not generate sufficient cash to service our indebtedness.** We have incurred net losses since inception. We reported a net loss of $24.8 million and $27.4 million for the fiscal years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We may continue to incur net losses for the foreseeable future, and we may not achieve or sustain profitability.

**Our professional services operations are currently margin-negative.** For fiscal year 2024, our professional services revenue was $18.7 million and our professional services cost of revenue was $18.8 million, resulting in a negative professional services gross margin of approximately (0.5)%. Although we expect professional services gross margins to improve as implementation processes scale and standardize, there can be no assurance that professional services will become profitable or that we will achieve our target professional services gross margins of 15% to 20%.

**We depend on a limited number of customers for a significant portion of our revenue.** Our top 10 customers represented approximately 31.7% of our total revenue in fiscal year 2024, and our largest customer, Harmon Industries Inc., represented approximately 8.2% of our total revenue. The loss of, or a material reduction in purchases by, one or more of these customers could materially and adversely affect our revenue and results of operations.

**We face intense competition and may lack sufficient resources to remain competitive.** The market for supply chain optimization software is competitive, with well-capitalized incumbents and point-solution vendors. We may not be able to compete effectively.

**We are subject to intellectual property litigation that could materially and adversely affect our business.** On September 12, 2024, Optivance, Inc. filed a patent infringement lawsuit against us in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831), alleging that certain features of our SupplyMind platform infringe U.S. Patent No. 11,284,107, relating to "machine-learning-based inventory forecasting." We believe the claims are without merit and intend to vigorously defend against them. However, an adverse judgment could result in damages that our outside patent counsel currently estimate could range from $5.0 million to $20.0 million, and could result in injunctive relief requiring us to redesign or cease offering certain features. In addition, an adverse judgment in excess of $5.0 million that remains undischarged for 60 days could constitute an event of default under our term loan credit agreement, which could result in the acceleration of our indebtedness. See "Risk Factors—Risks Related to Our Indebtedness" and "Description of Indebtedness."

**Our intellectual property is pledged as collateral under our credit agreement.** Substantially all of our assets, including our intellectual property (consisting of our 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, 8 issued foreign patents, and our registered trademarks "Crestline," "Crestline Nexus," and "SupplyMind"), are pledged as collateral to secure our obligations under our term loan. In the event of a default, the lender could foreclose on our intellectual property, which would severely and adversely affect our business.

**We depend on key personnel, including our co-founders.** Our success depends on the continued service of our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy, our Chief Executive Officer, and Katerina [●] Litvak, our Chief Technology Officer. The loss of the services of either co-founder could adversely affect our business. In addition, the departure of Mr. Ramaswamy as Chief Executive Officer could constitute a "change of control" under our term loan credit agreement unless a successor reasonably acceptable to the lender is appointed within 90 days, which could result in the acceleration of our indebtedness. See "Description of Indebtedness."

**If we fail to maintain effective internal control over financial reporting, we may not be able to accurately report our financial results.** As an emerging growth company, we are not required to obtain an attestation from our independent registered public accounting firm on our internal control over financial reporting. If we fail to maintain effective internal controls, we could be subject to regulatory scrutiny and a loss of investor confidence.

**Our ability to use our net operating loss carryforwards and certain other tax attributes may be limited.** As of December 31, 2024, we had approximately $228.0 million of federal net operating loss ("NOL") carryforwards and approximately $166.0 million of state NOL carryforwards. Our ability to use these NOLs may be limited if we experience an "ownership change" as defined in Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code, including in connection with this offering or other equity transactions. We have not yet completed a Section 382 study.

**We have identified a related party customer relationship that requires ongoing disclosure.** Vantara Logistics, Inc., a portfolio company of Graymont Ventures (our largest pre-offering stockholder), is a customer under a SaaS license agreement entered into in April 2023 with an annual contract value of $1.2 million and a three-year term. Priya Venkatesh, a member of our Board of Directors, is the Managing Partner of Graymont Ventures and serves on the board of Vantara Logistics. This transaction was reviewed and approved by our Audit Committee, with Ms. Venkatesh recused. See "Certain Relationships and Related Party Transactions."

### Risks Related to Our Indebtedness

**Our term loan credit agreement contains restrictive covenants and events of default that could limit our flexibility and result in acceleration of our indebtedness.** Our term loan with Kestridge West Credit Partners requires us to maintain minimum liquidity of $30.0 million and an ARR-based leverage ratio not exceeding 2.0x. The credit agreement also contains a change-of-control provision, a key-person provision relating to our Chief Executive Officer, a requirement that we provide the lender with at least 30 days' prior written notice before any public offering, and a judgment default threshold of $5.0 million. Our failure to comply with these covenants could result in an event of default and the acceleration of our indebtedness, which would materially and adversely affect our financial condition. See "Description of Indebtedness."

### Risks Related to Our Class A Common Stock and This Offering

**Because of the ten-to-one voting ratio of our Class B common stock, our co-founders will control a majority of the combined voting power of our capital stock and will be able to control all matters submitted to a vote of our stockholders.** Our co-founders will hold all of our outstanding Class B common stock, representing approximately 71.8% of the combined voting power of our outstanding capital stock immediately following this offering. As a result, our co-founders will have the ability to control the outcome of all matters submitted to a vote of our stockholders, including the election of directors, amendments to our certificate of incorporation, and approval of significant corporate transactions. This concentrated voting control will limit the ability of holders of our Class A common stock to influence corporate matters.

**Our Class B common stock has no sunset provision.** Our Class B common stock will continue to have ten votes per share indefinitely, and will convert into Class A common stock only upon transfer to a non-permitted transferee or upon the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based or ownership-based sunset. As a result, the concentration of voting control with our co-founders will continue indefinitely. Certain major stock index providers may exclude companies with multi-class voting structures from their indices, which could limit the demand for, and the market price of, our Class A common stock.

**We are a "controlled company" under Nasdaq listing rules and may rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements.** Because our co-founders will control more than 50% of the voting power of our outstanding capital stock, we will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq listing rules. As a controlled company, we may elect to rely on exemptions from the Nasdaq requirements that a majority of our board of directors be comprised of independent directors, that we have a fully independent compensation committee, and that we have a fully independent nominating committee. As of the date of this prospectus, our Board of Directors consists of [●] members, [●] of whom are independent. If we rely on these exemptions, you will not have the same protections afforded to stockholders of companies that are subject to these corporate governance requirements.

**Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws contain provisions that may discourage acquisition proposals and limit the ability of stockholders to influence corporate matters.** These provisions include a classified (staggered) board of directors, the ability of the Board of Directors to fill vacancies, the elimination of the ability of holders of Class A common stock to act by written consent, limitations on the ability of stockholders to call special meetings, advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations, a requirement of a 66⅔% supermajority vote to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws, and director removal only for cause and upon a 66⅔% vote.

**Our exclusive forum provisions may limit the ability of stockholders to bring claims in certain judicial forums.** Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation provides that, unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act. These provisions may limit the ability of stockholders to bring claims in state court or in the courts of their choosing.

**You will experience immediate and substantial dilution.** The assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated price range) is substantially higher than the pro forma net tangible book value per share of our Class A common stock immediately following this offering. As a result, you will experience immediate and substantial dilution of $19.85 per share. See "Dilution."

**Future sales of our Class A common stock, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.** Substantial shares of our Class A common stock will be eligible for sale following the expiration of the 180-day lock-up period, including shares issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options (14,750,000 options at a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share) and the vesting of outstanding RSUs (3,200,000 RSUs). In addition, holders of our preferred stock have registration rights commencing 180 days after this offering. These sales could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline.

**We do not intend to pay dividends on our Class A common stock.** We have never declared or paid any cash dividends on our capital stock. We do not anticipate paying any cash dividends in the foreseeable future. In addition, our term loan credit agreement restricts our ability to pay dividends or repurchase equity without the lender's consent. See "Description of Indebtedness" and "Dividend Policy."

## USE OF PROCEEDS

We are offering [●] shares of our Class A common stock. We estimate that the net proceeds from this offering will be approximately $[●] million (or approximately $[●] million if the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full), based on an assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share (the midpoint of the estimated price range), after deducting the underwriting discount of 6.5% and estimated offering expenses payable by us of approximately $4.8 million.

## DILUTION

The net tangible book value of our Class A common stock as of December 31, 2024 was approximately $(3.29) per share. Net tangible book value per share is determined by dividing our total tangible assets (total assets of $240.1 million less intangible assets of $4.1 million) less total liabilities of $143.1 million, by the number of shares of common stock outstanding as of December 31, 2024 (52,000,000 shares), resulting in net tangible assets of $92.9 million, or $1.79 per share.

After giving effect to (i) the automatic conversion of all 35,416,667 shares of outstanding redeemable convertible preferred stock into shares of Class A common stock on a one-for-one basis and the reclassification of $385.0 million from mezzanine equity to permanent stockholders' equity, and (ii) the sale of 15,000,000 shares of Class A common stock in this offering at an assumed initial public offering price of $24.00 per share, after deducting the underwriting discount and estimated offering expenses, our pro forma net tangible book value as of December 31, 2024 would have been approximately $4.15 per share. This represents an immediate increase in pro forma net tangible book value of $3.09 per share to existing stockholders and immediate dilution of $19.85 per share to new investors purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering, as illustrated in the following table:

| | |
|---|---|
| Assumed initial public offering price per share | $24.00 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share before this offering | $1.06 |
| Increase per share attributable to this offering | $3.09 |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering | $4.15 |
| Dilution per share to new investors | $19.85 |

If the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full, the pro forma net tangible book value per share after this offering would be approximately $4.36, and the dilution to new investors would be approximately $19.64 per share.

The foregoing dilution analysis does not give effect to (i) 14,750,000 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon exercise of outstanding stock options at a weighted-average exercise price of $8.42 per share, (ii) 3,200,000 shares of Class A common stock issuable upon vesting and settlement of outstanding RSUs, (iii) 550,000 shares remaining available for future grant under our 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, and (iv) 2,500,000 shares reserved under our 2024 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. To the extent these options or RSUs are exercised or vest, or additional awards are granted, there will be further dilution to new investors.

## PLAN OF DISTRIBUTION

We have entered into an underwriting agreement with Pinnacle Ridge Capital, as representative of the several underwriters, and Stonebridge Greer & Co., pursuant to which we have agreed to sell to the underwriters the shares of Class A common stock offered by this prospectus. The underwriting agreement will provide that the obligations of the underwriters are subject to customary conditions, including the effectiveness of the registration statement, the receipt of comfort letters from our independent auditor, opinions of counsel, and no material adverse change in our business.

The underwriters are offering the shares of Class A common stock, subject to receipt and acceptance, and subject to the underwriters' right to reject any order in whole or in part. The underwriting discount is 6.5% of the gross proceeds, allocated as a management fee of 20%, an underwriting fee of 20%, and a selling concession of 60%.

**Over-Allotment Option.** We have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2,250,000 shares of Class A common stock (15% of the base offering) at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discount, solely to cover over-allotments, if any.

**Stabilization.** The underwriters may engage in over-allotment sales, stabilizing transactions, and penalty bids in accordance with Regulation M under the Exchange Act. These activities may have the effect of raising or maintaining the market price of our Class A common stock above independent market levels or preventing or retarding a decline in the market price.

**Lock-Up Agreements.** Each of our executive officers, directors, and stockholders beneficially owning 5% or more of our outstanding capital stock immediately prior to this offering has agreed, subject to customary exceptions, not to offer, sell, or otherwise dispose of any shares of our capital stock or securities convertible into or exercisable for our capital stock for a period of 180 days following the date of the final prospectus, without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital. The lock-up agreements must be executed by the applicable fund-level entities that hold the shares, and not merely by the individual directors who serve as representatives on our Board. See "Certain Relationships and Related Party Transactions" and "Principal Stockholders."

**Listing.** We have applied to list our Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "CRST."

## DESCRIPTION OF CAPITAL STOCK

The following description summarizes the material terms of our capital stock. This description is qualified in its entirety by reference to our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and amended and restated bylaws, each of which will become effective immediately prior to the closing of this offering, copies of which are filed as exhibits to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

### General

Upon the closing of this offering, our amended and restated certificate of incorporation will become effective. It will authorize 1,500,000,000 shares of capital stock, consisting of 1,200,000,000 shares of Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; 200,000,000 shares of Class B common stock, par value $0.0001 per share; and 100,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value $0.0001 per share.

### Conversion of Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock

Upon the closing of this offering, all outstanding shares of our Series A, Series B, Series C, and Series D redeemable convertible preferred stock (an aggregate of 35,416,667 shares) will automatically convert into shares of Class A common stock on a one-for-one basis, and the $385.0 million aggregate liquidation preference will be reclassified from mezzanine equity to permanent stockholders' equity. No shares of preferred stock will remain outstanding.

### Class A Common Stock

Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to one vote per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders. Holders of Class A common stock are entitled to receive dividends when, as, and if declared by our Board of Directors, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock. Upon any liquidation, dissolution, or winding up, holders of Class A common stock are entitled to share ratably in the assets remaining after payment of liabilities and any preferred stock amounts, on an equal per share basis with holders of Class B common stock.

### Class B Common Stock

Holders of Class B common stock are entitled to ten votes per share on all matters submitted to a vote of stockholders. Class B common stock is held exclusively by our co-founders, Arun Ramaswamy (12,200,000 shares) and Katerina [●] Litvak (8,600,000 shares), for an aggregate of 20,800,000 shares. Each share of Class B common stock is convertible at any time at the option of the holder into one share of Class A common stock, and will automatically convert into one share of Class A common stock upon (i) transfer to any person or entity other than a permitted transferee, or (ii) the death or incapacity of the holder. There is no time-based or ownership-based sunset on the Class B common stock. Other than with respect to voting and conversion, the rights of Class B common stock are identical to those of Class A common stock.

### Preferred Stock

Our Board of Directors is authorized to issue shares of preferred stock in one or more series and to fix the designations, powers, preferences, and rights of each series without further stockholder approval. The issuance of preferred stock could adversely affect the voting power of holders of Class A common stock and could be used as a method of discouraging, delaying, or preventing a change of control.

### Registration Rights

Holders of our outstanding preferred stock (which will convert into Class A common stock in this offering) are parties to an Investor Rights Agreement dated June 2023, pursuant to which they are entitled to demand registration rights and S-3 piggyback registration rights commencing 180 days after the completion of this offering, subject to the terms and conditions of the agreement. A copy of the Investor Rights Agreement is filed as an exhibit to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

### Anti-Takeover Provisions

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation and bylaws contain provisions that may discourage, delay, or prevent a change of control, including: a classified board of directors with staggered three-year terms; the ability of the Board of Directors to fill vacancies; the elimination of the ability of holders of Class A common stock to act by written consent; limitations on the ability of stockholders to call special meetings; advance notice requirements for stockholder proposals and nominations; a requirement of a 66⅔% supermajority vote to amend certain provisions of our charter and bylaws; and director removal only for cause and upon a 66⅔% vote. Our certificate of incorporation also renounces certain corporate opportunities that may be presented to directors who are affiliated with investment funds, including Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners.

### Exclusive Forum

Our amended and restated certificate of incorporation provides that the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware will be the sole and exclusive forum for certain state-law claims, and that the federal district courts of the United States will be the sole and exclusive forum for claims arising under the Securities Act, in each case unless we consent in writing to the selection of an alternative forum.

## DESCRIPTION OF INDEBTEDNESS

The following is a summary of the material terms of our term loan credit agreement with Kestridge West Credit Partners. This summary is qualified in its entirety by reference to the credit agreement, a copy of which is filed as an exhibit to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.

In [●] 2022, we entered into a senior secured term loan facility with Kestridge West Credit Partners providing for borrowings of up to $50.0 million. As of December 31, 2024, $40.0 million was outstanding under the facility. The facility matures in December 2027 and bears interest at a variable rate equal to SOFR plus 3.25%, subject to a SOFR floor of 1.00%. The facility amortizes in quarterly installments, with the remaining balance due at maturity. An exit fee of 1.00% of the outstanding principal is due upon repayment in full or at maturity.

**Security and Guarantee.** Our obligations under the credit agreement are secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including our accounts receivable, intellectual property, equipment, deposit accounts, and equity interests in our subsidiaries (limited to 65% of the voting equity of our foreign subsidiary, Crestline Software UK Ltd.). Crestline Software UK Ltd. provides a limited guarantee of our obligations, capped at £5.0 million.

**Financial Covenants.** We are required to maintain minimum liquidity (cash, cash equivalents, and eligible short-term investments) of not less than $30.0 million, and an ARR-based leverage ratio (total funded debt divided by ARR) not exceeding 2.0x, in each case tested quarterly. As of December 31, 2024, we were in compliance with all financial covenants, with liquidity of $137.0 million and a leverage ratio of approximately 0.19x.

**Change of Control.** A "change of control" under the credit agreement includes, among other things, the existing stockholders ceasing to hold at least 50% of the aggregate voting power of our outstanding capital stock, including following an initial public offering, and the cessation of Arun Ramaswamy as Chief Executive Officer unless a successor reasonably acceptable to the lender is appointed within 90 days. Based on the dual-class voting structure, our co-founders will retain approximately 71.8% of the voting power following this offering, and we do not believe this offering will trigger a change of control.

**Public Offering Notice.** We are required to provide the lender with written notice not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering, including the anticipated offering date, anticipated gross proceeds, anticipated use of proceeds, and a pro forma capitalization table. Failure to provide this notice within the required timeframe constitutes an event of default, subject to a 10-business-day cure period.

**Other Covenants and Events of Default.** The credit agreement contains customary affirmative and negative covenants, including limitations on additional indebtedness (exceeding $5.0 million without consent), liens, investments, restricted payments (including dividends and stock repurchases), and mergers and acquisitions. Events of default include cross-default to other indebtedness exceeding $2.0 million and judgments for the payment of money in excess of $5.0 million that remain undischarged for 60 days. Upon an event of default, the lender may accelerate all outstanding obligations and exercise remedies against the collateral.

## MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following discussion and analysis should be read together with our consolidated financial statements and the related notes included elsewhere in this prospectus. This discussion contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties. Our actual results could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements.

### Overview

We are an enterprise SaaS company providing an AI-driven supply chain optimization platform. We generate revenue from (i) subscription fees for access to our cloud-based platform, recognized ratably over the contractual term (typically one to three years), and (ii) professional services, recognized as services are delivered. For fiscal year 2024, subscription revenue was $168.6 million (90.0% of total revenue) and professional services revenue was $18.7 million (10.0% of total revenue). Total revenue was $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, an increase of 45.3% from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023. As of December 31, 2024, ARR was $211.6 million and our dollar-based net retention rate was 132%.

We have incurred net losses since inception. For fiscal years 2024 and 2023, we reported net losses of $24.8 million and $27.4 million, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, we had an accumulated deficit of $312.6 million. We have funded our operations primarily through the issuance of redeemable convertible preferred stock (an aggregate of $385.0 million across four series) and, to a lesser extent, revenue from operations and borrowings under our credit facility.

**Revenue.** Total revenue increased $58.4 million, or 45.3%, from $128.9 million in fiscal year 2023 to $187.3 million in fiscal year 2024, driven by new customer acquisition and expansion within our existing customer base. Subscription revenue increased $52.2 million, or 44.8%, and professional services revenue increased $6.2 million, or 49.6%.

**Gross Profit and Gross Margin.** Gross profit increased $43.5 million, or 49.7%, from $87.6 million in fiscal year 2023 to $131.1 million in fiscal year 2024. Gross margin increased from 67.9% to 70.0%. Subscription gross margin was 77.8% in fiscal year 2024. Professional services gross margin was approximately (0.5)% in fiscal year 2024, as professional services cost of revenue ($18.8 million) exceeded professional services revenue ($18.7 million).

**Operating Expenses.** Total operating expenses increased $40.4 million, or 34.0%, from $118.7 million in fiscal year 2023 to $159.1 million in fiscal year 2024, primarily due to increased headcount and stock-based compensation. Stock-based compensation expense was $38.2 million and $26.4 million in fiscal years 2024 and 2023, respectively. Fiscal year 2024 also included $1.2 million of restructuring charges related to a workforce reduction affecting approximately 46 employees in the third quarter of 2024.

**Net Loss.** Net loss decreased $2.6 million, from $27.4 million in fiscal year 2023 to $24.8 million in fiscal year 2024, reflecting improved operating leverage.

Adjusted EBITDA is not a substitute for GAAP net loss. Investors should be aware that our definition of Adjusted EBITDA may not be comparable to similarly titled measures used by other companies.
